Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. A Visual Introduction to Machine Learning (2015) 131 upvotes, 7 comments
  2. Mathematics Distillation Challenge – Equational Theories 85 upvotes, 4 comments
  3. A look inside Dialector, filmmaker Chris Marker's chatbot from 1988 58 upvotes, 4 comments
  4. Tree Search Distillation for Language Models Using PPO 72 upvotes, 7 comments
  5. Show HN: Signet – Autonomous wildfire tracking from satellite and weather data 31 upvotes, 2 comments
  6. Examples for the tcpdump and dig man pages 40 upvotes, 4 comments
  7. SBCL Fibers – Lightweight Cooperative Threads 127 upvotes, 23 comments
  8. Rack-mount hydroponics 236 upvotes, 51 comments
  9. Human Organ Atlas 25 upvotes, 2 comments
  10. Bumblebee queens breathe underwater to survive drowning 163 upvotes, 37 comments
  11. Pentagon expands oversight of Stars and Stripes, limits content 63 upvotes, 13 comments
  12. Generating All 32-Bit Primes (Part I) 34 upvotes, 6 comments
  13. Why Mathematica does not simplify sinh(arccosh(x)) 98 upvotes, 30 comments
  14. A most elegant TCP hole punching algorithm 140 upvotes, 50 comments
  15. Slicing Bezier Surfaces 20 upvotes, 4 comments
  16. Allow me to get to know you, mistakes and all 210 upvotes, 93 comments
  17. Centuries of selective breeding turned wild cabbage into different vegetables 82 upvotes, 32 comments
  18. A Theory of the World as run by large adult children 127 upvotes, 59 comments
  19. An ode to bzip 158 upvotes, 82 comments
  20. Show HN: Han – A Korean programming language written in Rust 190 upvotes, 104 comments
  21. Small U.S. town, big company. Can it weather the tariff Blizzard? (Digi-Key) 16 upvotes, 4 comments
  22. Ageless Linux – Software for humans of indeterminate age 716 upvotes, 468 comments
  23. $96 3D-printed rocket that recalculates its mid-air trajectory using a $5 sensor 209 upvotes, 135 comments
  24. IBM, sonic delay lines, and the history of the 80×24 display 18 upvotes, 6 comments
  25. 100 hour gap between a vibecoded prototype and a working product 54 upvotes, 31 comments
  26. The Appalling Stupidity of Spotify's AI DJ 248 upvotes, 204 comments
  27. How kernel anti-cheats work 248 upvotes, 209 comments
  28. MCP is dead; long live MCP 198 upvotes, 172 comments
  29. Treasure hunter freed from jail after refusing to turn over shipwreck gold 129 upvotes, 172 comments
  30. Hollywood Enters Oscars Weekend in Existential Crisis 12 upvotes, 10 comments

Last generated 10:30pm, 15 March 2026. Source here.
